response.answer:
Project membership matters because Alleato PM uses it as one of the core checks to decide what a person can see or change.

From the published help article **`docs/help/articles/app-permissions-and-visibility.md`**:
- “Alleato PM uses role, project membership, and admin checks to decide what a person can see or change.”
- A hidden page/button/record is most likely because:
  - the user is not assigned to the project,
  - the page is admin-only,
  - the action requires stronger permission,
  - the record is private or employee-sensitive,
  - or the source system hasn’t synced the expected record yet.

What I checked:
- App Expert artifact status: current and available.
- Feature registry: found **`app-permissions-and-visibility`**.
- Help article: read **`docs/help/articles/app-permissions-and-visibility.md`**.

What remains uncertain:
- I cannot verify the access rules for a specific user or project without a current route or project ID.
- I also can’t confirm any broader membership behavior beyond the documented permission logic above.

Related routes from the help article:
- `/user-management`
- `/[projectId]/user-management`
- `/settings`
